evals.prompt: Set up hooks for this project. Before creating or updating a spec, check that the git working directory is clean. After every implementation is done, run 'npm test' and fix any failures before closing the spec.
evals.expected_output: mini-sdd/mini-sdd.config.yml is created containing only two hook events: spec.pre with a git-clean-check instruction and implement.post with an npm-test instruction. All other events (context.pre, context.post, spec.post, implement.pre) are omitted from the file. The skill prints the final YAML and confirms it was saved.
expectations: mini-sdd/mini-sdd.config.yml is created
The file contains a spec.pre hook
The spec.pre instruction mentions 'git' or 'working directory' or 'clean' or 'uncommitted'
The file contains an implement.post hook
The implement.post instruction mentions 'npm test' or 'test'
context.pre, context.post, spec.post, and implement.pre are NOT present in the file
The skill shows the final written file content
The skill confirms the file was saved with a success message

evals.prompt: Update the hooks config. Keep the existing spec.pre hook. Add a new post-context hook: after writing context.md, update the root README.md Overview section to match the new product description.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads the existing mini-sdd.config.yml, shows a summary of the current hooks, then updates the file. The resulting config preserves the original spec.pre instruction and adds a new context.post entry about updating the README. The skill shows the final file content and confirms the save.
expectations: The skill reads and shows a summary of the current config before making changes
mini-sdd/mini-sdd.config.yml is updated (overwritten)
The file contains a context.post hook
The context.post instruction mentions 'README' or 'Overview'
The original spec.pre instruction is still present in the updated file
The skill shows the final written file content
The skill confirms the file was saved with a success message

evals.prompt: Initialize the mini-sdd config file, but I don't need any hooks right now.
evals.expected_output: mini-sdd/mini-sdd.config.yml is created with a minimal valid structure containing only a top-level 'hooks: {}' block (or equivalent). No hook event lists are written. The skill notes that no hooks are active and confirms the file was saved.
expectations: mini-sdd/mini-sdd.config.yml is created
The file is valid YAML (no syntax errors)
The file contains a top-level 'hooks' key
No hook event sublists are written (no spec.pre, implement.post, etc.)
The skill notes that no hooks are active or 0 hook events are configured
The skill confirms the file was saved with a success message
